test_name: March 2025 International Form C SAT Math Module 2
passage: How many solutions does the equation $2x-3=2x$ have?

test_name: March 2025 International Form C SAT Math Module 2
passage: In the linear function f, f(0) = 2 and f(7)= 2. Which equation defines f?

test_name: March 2025 International Form C SAT Math Module 2
passage: A rectangle has a length that is 92 times its width. The function y= (92w)(w) represents this situation, where y is the area, in square feet, of the rectangle and y > 0. Which of the following is the best interpretation of 92w in this context?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The length of the rectangle, in feet

choices.label: B
choices.text: The area of the rectangle, in square feet

choices.label: C
choices.text: The difference between the length and the width of the rectangle, in feet

choices.label: D
choices.text: The width of the rectangle, in feet

test_name: March 2025 International Form C SAT Math Module 2
passage:
$T(x)=67-8x$
The function T estimates the temperature, in degrees Fahrenheit (°F), in a certain city one day in January, where x is the number of hours after 10 a.m. and 1 $\le$ x $\le$ 5. According to the function, what was the estimated decrease in temperature each hour, in °F, in this city during this time period?

test_name: March 2025 International Form C SAT Math Module 2
passage: At a convention center, there are a total of 375 visitors. Each visitor is located in either room A, room B, or room C. If one of these visitors is selected at random, the probability of selecting a visitor who is located in room A is 0.68, and the probability of selecting a visitor who is located in room B is 0.24. How many visitors are located in room C?

test_name: March 2025 International Form C SAT Math Module 2
passage: An object is launched from a height of 121 feet above the ground. A quadratic function f models the height above the ground, in feet, of the object t seconds after it is launched. According to the model, 2 seconds after the object is launched, it reaches a maximum height of 185 feet above the ground, which equation defines f?

test_name: March 2025 International Form C SAT Math Module 2
passage:
$7y=-6x+2,550$
$24x-28y=1,800$
The solution to the given system of equations is (x, y). What is the value of x + y?

test_name: March 2025 International Form C SAT Math Module 2
passage: To study fluctuations in leaf water potential, samples of wood were taken from 21 trees and cut in the shape of a cube. The length of the edge of one of these cubes is 3.000 centimeters. This cube has a density of 0.220 grams per cubic centimeter. What is the mass of this cube, in grams?

test_name: March 2025 International Form C SAT Math Module 2
passage:
$-2|3x+7|+8=-6$
What are all solutions to the given equation?

test_name: March 2025 International Form C SAT Math Module 2
passage: The graph shows a linear relationship between x and y. Which equation represents this relationship, where R is a positive constant?

test_name: March 2025 International Form C SAT Math Module 2
passage: The function f is defined by f(x)=-10(3) x + $\frac{1}{k}$, where k is a constant. If y= f(x) is graphed in the xy-plane, what is the y-coordinate of the y-intercept of the graph?

test_name: March 2025 International Form C SAT Math Module 2
passage: The shaded region shown represents the solutions to rx + ty $\ge$ -77, where r and t are constants. What is the value of r+t?

test_name: March 2025 International Form C SAT Math Module 2
passage:
$3x=45y-27$
One of the two equations in a system of linear equations is given. The system has no solution, Which equation could be the second equation in this system?

test_name: March 2025 International Form C SAT Math Module 2
passage: A cooking school is offering a promotion where the first class is free, the second class is half off the regular price, and the remaining classes are regularly priced. if the regular price of a class is \$22.80, which function f gives the total cost, in dollars, of x classes taken using this promotion,where x $\ge$ 2?

test_name: March 2025 International Form C SAT Math Module 2
passage: The function f is defined by f(x)= 6x + 14. What is the y-coordinate of the y-intercept of the graph of y= f(x) in the xy-plane?

test_name: March 2025 International Form C SAT Math Module 2
passage: The graph of y= f(x)+ 2 is shown. Which equation defines function f?

test_name: March 2025 International Form C SAT Math Module 2
passage: A square map has a side length of 55 inches, and 1 inch on the map represents an actual distance of 13 miles. A smaller version of the same map is printed as a square with the side length 70% shorter than the side length of the previous map. On the smaller map, which of the following is closest to the actual distance, in miles, represented by 1 inch?

test_name: March 2025 International Form C SAT Math Module 2
passage:
$y=x-c$
$y=-4(x-8)^{2}$
In the given system of equations, c is a constant. The system has two distinct real solutions. Which of the following could be the value of c?

test_name: March 2025 International Form C SAT Math Module 2
passage: In triangle ABC, the measure of angle A is 52°and AC= 35. In triangle PQR, the measure of angle P is 52°and PR= 105. Which additional piece of information is sufficient to prove that triangle ABC is similar to triangle PQR?

choices.label: C
choices.text: The measures of angle B and angle R are 34°and 94°, respectively.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The measures of angle B and angle Q are 52°and 34°, respectively.

test_name: March 2025 International Form C SAT Math Module 2
passage: The perimeter of an equilateral triangle is 876 centimeters. The three vertices of the triangle lie on a circle. The radius of the circle is w $\sqrt{3}$ centimeters. What is the value of w?

test_name: March 2025 International Form C SAT Math Module 2
passage: The function f is defined by f(x)= $\frac{|x|}{a}$ - 14, where a < 0. What is the product of f(15a) and f(8a)?

test_name: March 2025 International Form C SAT Math Module 2
passage: The function f is defined by $f(x)=ab^{\frac{\pi}{n}}$, where a, b, and n are constants, and b and n are integers. If f(4)=5 and f(7)= 135, what is the value of f(9)?
